prerequisites: An API key retrieved from the Account Settings page in the Campaign Monitor web interface.
The username (your API key) is required. The password field can be left blank or set to a dummy value, since it is not used for authentication.

authMethods.name: API key (HTTP Basic Authentication)
authMethods.summary: Campaign Monitor authenticates API requests with HTTP Basic Auth where the API key is supplied as the username. Set the connector's `username` field to your Campaign Monitor API key. The `password` field is not used for authentication and may be left blank or set to a dummy value such as 'x'.

setupSteps.title: Retrieve your Campaign Monitor API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Campaign Monitor and open the Account Settings page to find your API key (for example, a value like 'dklkmwlmkdy7qwd98y98y98y8d68d9').

setupSteps.title: Set the username and password fields
setupSteps.body: Enter your API key in the required `username` field. Campaign Monitor uses HTTP Basic Authentication with the API key as the username, so the `password` field is optional and may be left blank or set to a dummy value like 'x', as it is not used for authentication.

setupSteps.title: Set an optional start date
setupSteps.body: Optionally set `start_date` to bound how far back data is replicated. Leave it empty to use the connector's default behavior.

setupSteps.title: Be aware of rate limiting
setupSteps.body: Campaign Monitor enforces API rate limits and returns an HTTP 429 status when exceeded. Responses include X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset headers indicating the allowed request count, the requests remaining, and the seconds until the limit resets.
